body_txt: Lilliputians are humanoid characters whose main characteristic is that they are very, very small. Like, the size of a bottle. Or the size of your thumb. Or the size of an ant . Little People may be small, but they are at least human-sized. Lilliputians are too small: you just can't interact with the world in a normal capacity at that scale. Every insect is a threat, every step up a staircase is a colossal climb, and no big person ever watches where they're stepping. Life for these guys is usually pretty tough, but they might get some levity if there's enough of them to make up a Mouse World . The most comfortable version is when the Lilliputians are from Unknown Lands , who have no idea that they are comparitavely tiny to the "normal people", as is the case with the original Lilliputians from Gulliver's Travels . Lilliputian Warriors is a subtrope, for when these guys kick ass despite fitting in the palm of a hand. Contrast Our Giants Are Bigger , to whom we are the Lilliputians. Fairies and pixies are pretty often Lilliputians, in modern depictions at least. They usually have wings that make their life way easier, though. Meanwhile, The Fair Folk , House Fey , and Christmas Elves might be this or Little People depending on the depiction. Note that regular-sized people who shrink down and then revert to normal size fall under Incredible Shrinking Man ; this trope is for naturally small humanoids. Also, Not to Be Confused with Liverpudlians .
